basics.description:
**SpyCloud Sentinel Supreme v12.0** — Deploy the most comprehensive darknet identity threat intelligence integration for Microsoft Sentinel.

This solution deploys:
- **Data Connector** — 13 CCF pollers ingesting SpyCloud data across 15 custom tables
- **38 Analytics Rules** — Credential exposure, infostealer detection, cross-platform correlation
- **16 Hunting Queries** — Proactive threat hunting across all SpyCloud data
- **13 Logic App Playbooks** — MDE isolation, CA password reset, enrichment, investigation
- **Azure Function App** — Centralized enrichment with Identity Risk Score engine
- **Key Vault** — Secure API key management
- **Workbook** — SOC operational dashboard
- **Security Copilot Plugin** — 28+ KQL skills + AI agent

For setup help, contact [integrations@spycloud.com](mailto:integrations@spycloud.com) or visit [portal.spycloud.com](https://portal.spycloud.com).

options.text: Select or create the Log Analytics workspace where SpyCloud data will be ingested. All tables, rules, playbooks, and workbooks deploy to this workspace.

elements.name: createNewWorkspace
elements.label: Workspace
elements.toolTip: Create a new workspace or select an existing one. If you already have Sentinel enabled on a workspace, select 'Use existing'.

elements.name: workspace
elements.label: Workspace Name
elements.toolTip: Name of the Log Analytics workspace. Must be unique within the resource group.

constraints.validationMessage: 4-63 characters, letters, numbers, and hyphens only.

options.text:
Enter your SpyCloud Enterprise API key below. This is the ONLY key needed during deployment — it's used to authenticate the Function App, Key Vault, and enrichment playbooks.

After deployment, go to the Data Connector page to add product-specific API keys (Compass, SIP, Investigations, etc.) — those keys are stored persistently in the connector and can be updated anytime without redeploying.

elements.name: apiKey
elements.toolTip: Your SpyCloud Enterprise API key from portal.spycloud.com → Settings → API Keys. This key is used by the Function App, enrichment playbooks, and the Breach Watchlist + Breach Catalog pollers. It's stored securely in Azure Key Vault.

options.text:
**How API keys work in this solution:**

1. **Enterprise key** (entered above) → stored in Key Vault → used by Function App + playbooks
2. **Product keys** (Compass, SIP, etc.) → entered on the Data Connector page after deployment → stored in the CCF connector → used by data pollers

You never need to enter the same key twice. The Enterprise key handles all enrichment. Product keys handle data ingestion.

All 38 analytics rules deploy as templates. After deployment, go to Sentinel → Analytics → Rule Templates → filter 'SpyCloud' to review and enable rules individually. Rules are NOT active until you enable them — this gives you full control over which detections to activate.

elements.name: spycloudSeverityThreshold
elements.label: Minimum Severity for Infostealer Rules
elements.toolTip:
Controls the severity threshold for infostealer detection rules.

• Severity 2 = Public breach credential
• Severity 5 = Private/underground breach
• Severity 20 = Infostealer malware infection
• Severity 25 = Infostealer with session cookies/tokens

Recommended: 20 (infostealers). Lower values generate more alerts but may include less actionable breach data.

elements.name: enableUEBA
elements.label: Enable UEBA (User and Entity Behavior Analytics)
elements.toolTip: Enables Sentinel's built-in UEBA engine which creates behavioral baselines for users and entities. SpyCloud correlation rules use UEBA anomaly scores to detect compromised users exhibiting unusual behavior. Recommended for all environments.

elements.name: enableAnomalies
elements.label: Enable Anomaly Detection
elements.toolTip: Enables Sentinel's ML-based anomaly detection. Works with SpyCloud data to identify unusual patterns in credential exposure and infection rates.

options.text:
Playbooks are Logic Apps that automate incident response. Each deploys as an Azure resource with a system-assigned managed identity. After deployment, you may need to authorize the managed identity in Entra ID → Enterprise Applications.

All playbooks use the Enterprise API key stored in Key Vault — no additional key entry needed.

elements.name: enableMdePlaybook
elements.label: MDE Device Isolation — Auto-isolate infected devices in Microsoft Defender for Endpoint
elements.toolTip: When a SpyCloud infostealer incident fires (severity 20+), this playbook searches MDE for the infected device by machine ID and initiates full or selective isolation. Requires MDE P2 license. The playbook logs all actions to Spycloud_MDE_Logs_CL for audit.

elements.name: enableCaPlaybook
elements.label: Conditional Access — Force password reset and revoke sessions via Entra ID
elements.toolTip: When a SpyCloud credential exposure incident fires, this playbook forces a password reset, revokes all active sessions, and optionally adds the user to a high-risk Conditional Access group. Requires Entra ID P1+. Logs to SpyCloud_ConditionalAccessLogs_CL.

elements.name: enableCredResponsePlaybook
elements.label: Credential Response — Send Teams/Slack/email notifications on critical exposures
elements.toolTip: Sends rich notification cards to Microsoft Teams, Slack, or email when high-severity credential exposures are detected. Includes exposure details, risk score, and one-click remediation links. Configure notification channels in the Notifications step.

elements.name: enableMdeBlocklistPlaybook
elements.label: MDE Blocklist — Submit SpyCloud IOCs to Defender threat indicators
elements.toolTip: Submits malware C2 URLs and infected IPs from SpyCloud data as custom indicators in MDE. Runs on a schedule to keep the blocklist current. Requires MDE P2.

elements.name: enableTiEnrichmentPlaybook
elements.label: TI Enrichment — Cross-reference with VirusTotal, AbuseIPDB, GreyNoise
elements.toolTip: Enriches SpyCloud incidents with third-party threat intelligence. Checks malware hashes against VirusTotal, IP reputation against AbuseIPDB, and scanner classification against GreyNoise. Optional: requires VirusTotal API key (free tier available).

options.text: Enrichment playbooks call the SpyCloud API when incidents fire to add fresh, entity-specific breach context to incident comments. They use the Enterprise API key from Key Vault — no separate key entry needed. Each playbook auto-triggers via Sentinel automation rules.

elements.name: enableEnrichmentPlaybooks
elements.label: Enable all enrichment playbooks (recommended)
elements.toolTip: Deploys enrichment playbooks for email, domain, IP, username, and catalog lookups. Each triggers automatically when matching entities appear in SpyCloud incidents. Also deploys the Full Investigation orchestration playbook for Critical incidents.

options.text:
The SpyCloud Enrichment Function App provides centralized API enrichment with the Identity Risk Score engine. It deploys as a serverless Azure Function (Consumption plan — first 1M executions/month free) with Key Vault for API key storage.

The Risk Score (0-100) combines exposure severity, credential availability, stolen cookies, device infections, temporal decay, and remediation credit into a single metric. It can be pushed to Entra ID custom security attributes for Conditional Access policy enforcement.

elements.name: enableFunctionApp
elements.label: Deploy SpyCloud Enrichment Function App with Identity Risk Score (recommended)
elements.toolTip: Deploys: Function App (Python 3.11, Consumption plan), Key Vault (API key storage), Storage Account, Application Insights. The Function App provides 17 endpoints including risk scoring, enrichment, investigation orchestration, and reporting.

elements.name: enrichmentDailyLimit
elements.label: Daily Enrichment API Call Limit
elements.toolTip: Maximum SpyCloud API calls per day across all enrichment playbooks and the Function App. Prevents exceeding your SpyCloud API quota. Default 200 handles approximately 50-100 incidents per day. Increase for high-volume SOCs after confirming with SpyCloud support.

options.text: Configure notification channels for SpyCloud alerts. At least one channel is recommended for critical exposure alerts.

elements.name: teamsWebhook
elements.label: Microsoft Teams Webhook URL (optional)
elements.toolTip: Incoming webhook URL for a Teams channel. Create one in Teams → Channel → Connectors → Incoming Webhook. Critical SpyCloud alerts will post rich notification cards with exposure details and remediation links.

elements.name: slackWebhook
elements.label: Slack Webhook URL (optional)
elements.toolTip: Slack incoming webhook URL. Create one at api.slack.com → Your Apps → Incoming Webhooks.

elements.name: notificationEmail
elements.label: Notification Email Address (optional)
elements.toolTip: Email address for critical SpyCloud alerts. Typically a SOC distribution list or security team inbox.

options.text: These settings are optional and have sensible defaults. Most deployments can skip this step.

elements.name: retentionInDays
elements.label: Data Retention (days)
elements.toolTip: How long to retain SpyCloud data in Log Analytics. Longer retention enables better trend analysis but increases storage costs. 90 days is the Log Analytics default. 365 days recommended for compliance.

elements.name: tagEnvironment
elements.label: Environment Tag
elements.toolTip: Tag value for the 'environment' tag applied to all deployed resources. Examples: production, staging, development, poc.

elements.name: tagOwner
elements.label: Owner Tag
elements.toolTip: Tag value for the 'owner' tag applied to all deployed resources. Typically the team or person responsible.
